basis: Open, versioned aggregation + narrative-prompt heuristics for kanae fundFlowEdge assembly + flowNarrative generation over the global open-government fiscal corpus (ADR-2605263900: USAspending / EU FTS / IMF SDMX / World Bank / OECD / UN + JP 予算書 / 政府調達) and the danjo cross-reference graph (ADR-2605301600 + ADR-2605302245). Each method conforms to com.etzhayyim.kanae.methodNote. CONSTITUTIONAL: every method is NON-adjudicating (G4) — it describes a FACTUAL aggregation or flow pattern only, never a finding of wrongdoing; knownFalsePositiveModes are mandatory and document why a hit / pattern is NOT, by itself, evidence of a crime / 不正. Method = open (G6); the public audits the aggregation, not only the picture. Narrative generation is Murakumo-only (G7).
notes: DRAFT / 雛形. Every threshold + aggregation choice here is a placeholder planning figure pending (a) Council Lv6+ ≥3 attestation and (b) calibration against real pinned gov.dataset.* + danjo records. Do NOT treat as authoritative until status flips to 'attested' and councilAttestation lists ≥3 Lv6+ DIDs.

A method produces kanae.fundFlowEdge datoms and/or a kanae.flowNarrative (nonAdjudicatingNotice=true, ≥2 sourceRecordCids, this methodNoteCid, murakumoInferenceAttestation). It is a FACT about public fiscal records, NOT a verdict. Legal characterization, if ever sought, routes to external counsel via chigiri + Public Fund (G4).

Visualization of any flow is aggregate-first (G10); a named-party element is gated separately (source already names the party + severity-gated + Council-reviewed + 1 SBT = 1 vote). These methods do not authorize naming.
A visualization may imply nothing the flowNarrative does not factually state. Sankey thickness / treemap area / globe arcs are descriptive magnitudes only, never wrongdoing scores.

methods.title: Assemble the domestic fiscal chain appropriation → outlay → subaward → procurement-award → recipient
methods.definition: Join danjo budget_ledger datoms (appropriation ↔ outlay ↔ recipientLei ↔ program) with danjo procurement_graph datoms (authority ↔ award ↔ awardeeLei ↔ amount) on shared recipientLei / awardeeLei + fiscal period, per jurisdiction, into a connected fundFlowEdge chain. This is a FACTUAL reconstruction of the public spending chain; gaps in the chain are descriptive (e.g. an outlay whose appropriation line is not present in the pinned corpus) — see knownFalsePositiveModes.
knownFalsePositiveModes: Corpus coverage gaps — the pinned bulk archive may not include every appropriation/outlay line, so a missing chain link reflects data availability, NOT a concealed flow.
Cross-fiscal-year carryover — an outlay in FY(n) may draw on an FY(n-1) appropriation, breaking a naive same-period join.
LEI/entity resolution misses — a recipient without an LEI (small or foreign entity) may appear as a chain terminus when subawards in fact continue.
Reorganization of program codes between fiscal years — a program rename is not a redirected flow.

methods.title: Assemble inter-governmental transfer / aid / loan flows donor-juris → recipient-juris
methods.definition: From gov.dataset.statisticsObservation (IMF SDMX / World Bank / OECD.Stat / UN data), assemble donor-jurisdiction ↔ recipient-jurisdiction ↔ instrument ↔ amount ↔ period fundFlowEdge datoms. instrument ∈ {grant, loan, SDR-allocation, aid-disbursement, repayment, quota-subscription}. This is a FACTUAL aggregation of published inter-governmental statistics; magnitudes are reporting figures from the IGO, NOT independently audited by kanae.
knownFalsePositiveModes: Double-counting across IGO sources — the same flow may be reported by both OECD DAC and the World Bank with different classifications; dedup is heuristic.
Currency normalization error — converting to a common currency at a period-average rate distorts flows denominated in volatile currencies.
Reporting lag / revision — IGO statistics are revised; an early-vintage figure is not a final figure.
State-aligned reporting (CN-class, G13) — figures carry stateAlignedFlag and are NOT treated as independently verified.

methods.title: Aggregate recipient concentration within an authority / program (descriptive, aggregate-first)
methods.definition: For a (contractingAuthority OR program, fiscalPeriod), aggregate total flow by recipient class and compute a concentration measure (e.g. top-5 recipient share, HHI) for visualization as a treemap. This is a DESCRIPTIVE distribution of public spending; high concentration is lawful and common in specialized markets — see knownFalsePositiveModes. Aggregate-first (G10): recipient classes, not individuals, unless the source already names them and Council review applies.
knownFalsePositiveModes: Naturally concentrated markets — defense, aerospace, large-infrastructure procurement legitimately have few eligible suppliers.
Aggregation-unit choice changes the picture — measuring at program vs authority vs sub-agency level yields different concentration; the chosen unit is disclosed, not the only valid view.
A dominant recipient may be a pass-through (a prime contractor that subawards widely), so prime-level concentration overstates ultimate concentration.
Concentration is a distribution fact, NOT evidence of steering or wrongdoing (G4).

methods.title: Murakumo-only narrative prompt for a fundFlowEdge subgraph (factual, non-adjudicating)
methods.definition: Given a fundFlowEdge subgraph + its ≥2 sourceRecordCids, generate a FACTUAL natural-language description on the Murakumo fleet (G7): what flowed, from which authority/jurisdiction, to which recipient class/jurisdiction, in what amount and period, citing the source records. The prompt template FORBIDS legal-conclusion language and any assertion of crime / 不正 / violation (G4); it instructs the model to describe magnitudes and routes only and to surface knownFalsePositiveModes as caveats. Output is a kanae.flowNarrative with nonAdjudicatingNotice=true + murakumoInferenceAttestation.
knownFalsePositiveModes: LLM hallucination of an amount or endpoint — mitigated by ≥2 sourceRecordCids (G5) + Council review at R2 before publication.
Tone leakage toward insinuation — the prompt forbids legal-conclusion language; any narrative implying wrongdoing is a G4 violation and must be rejected.
Over-summarization that drops a material caveat — knownFalsePositiveModes from the assembly method MUST be carried into the narrative.
A factual narrative is NOT an allegation; downstream consumers (ossekai / kataribe) inherit the nonAdjudicatingNotice.
